# Synthesis summary (auto-generated by synthesize.py)

Evidence rows: 59 from 34 studies. Rows verified against primary sources: 10 (5 studies); the rest are flagged `verified=false` pending PDF access (see HANDOFF.md).

Method: vote counting + exact binomial sign test per claim family; descriptive normalization (relative % change) where baselines exist. Formal pooling deferred — variances are unreported in this literature.

## C1: instruction-tuning/RLHF degrades token-probability calibration

- Studies supporting: **2** | contradicting: **0** | sign-test p = 0.5000
- Effect sizes: median |rel change| 567.0% [range 176.9%, 957.1%]
- Rows: 2303.08774, 2509.20088

## C2: preference-based methods beat SFT on abstention/truthfulness quality

- Studies supporting: **3** | contradicting: **0** | sign-test p = 0.2500
- Effect sizes: median |rel change| 5.6% [range 1.6%, 17.5%]
- Rows: 2401.13275, 2404.14723, jmir-e76048

## C3: preference optimization reduces SFT abstention over-refusal

- Studies supporting: **1** | contradicting: **0** | sign-test p = 1.0000
- Effect sizes: median |rel change| 40.0% [range -45.5%, -27.7%]
- Rows: reanalysis-2401.13275

## C4: scale alone does not produce epistemic humility (sycophancy/self-knowledge)

- Studies supporting: **4** | contradicting: **0** | sign-test p = 0.1250
- Effect sizes: median |rel change| 41.5% [range -44.8%, 80.0%]
- Rows: 2109.07958, 2212.09251, 2305.18153, 2308.03958

## C5: targeted training interventions improve humility metrics

- Studies supporting: **10** | contradicting: **0** | sign-test p = 0.0020
- Effect sizes: median |rel change| 38.8% [range -80.6%, 40.4%]
- Rows: 2308.03958, 2311.08401, 2311.08596, 2311.09677, 2312.07000, 2402.00367, 2405.01525, 2405.21028, 2410.09724, 2505.01997

## Variance-aware rows (the only ones in the corpus)

- SycEval: sycophancy rate = 58.19 (persistence 78.5% (CI 77.2-79.8) — rare CI)
- Base models not sycophantic: % match user view = 52.6 (CI 52.3-53.0; sycophancy appears only after feedback tuning)
- Clinical SFT vs DPO: clinical reasoning accuracy = nan (p=0.003 — rare p-value; Mistral +7% p=0.004)
